reply: (1) This device is an AutomationDirect GS10 Variable Frequency Drive, used to control motor speed. (2) The most likely cause is a GFCI tripping due to an electrical fault, possibly a ground fault. (3) The tech should check the drive for any visible damage and ensure the GFCI is reset. If the GFCI keeps tripping, the drive may need to be inspected for internal faults.
